column10: Good spot if you're on a surfing trip. Leave the partner and kids and have a surf. My room was very basic with no bar fridge which I think is must to keep milk and water cold. Also walls between basic rooms are wafer thin. My issue is not so much with the property as some of the guests on my stay. It was nyepi time so guests expected to be silent and respectful yet many let their kids run riot screaming and yelling all day. Pool was available on this day but was small and full of kids so go figure.

column10: "Great location of this hotel right by the beach. Really lovely place to sit and have the delicious breakfast they served at the hotel. Room was clean
column11: shower good and bed comfortable enough. Bathroom is outside which we loved. Staff was super helpful and friendly. Balian Beach is a beautiful place. So much more quiet than other places we have been to in Bali. We absolutely loved it. At the start we weren't sure but now its one of the highlights of our trip even though we are no surfers. I wanted to take lessons but the waves were pretty darn high so didn't dare :). We went for twice a day for yoga with Niki @balianspirityoga that was a short walk from the hotel. Yoga at the hotel was not happening when we were there as too quiet (end of feb) but Niki is a fantastic teacher so glad it wasn't happening at the hotel or I wouldn't have met Niki. We finished each night at Rekis Warung a brilliant spot for sunset and delicious local foods. Make sure to bring cash to Balian as many places do not take cards and the nearest cashpoint don't accept most of the European cards. We had to take a taxi for 30 minutes to get to a cashpoint that took our cards."
